---
title: "Comparing eigengene networks between lines, control."
format: html
date: "2026-05-26"
bibliography: ../../../refs-stat-anal.bib
execute:
  fig-format: png
  fig.dpi: 300
---

## Rationale

The aim of this script is to compare the consensus eigengene networks between data sets (such comparison
is often called differential analysis). Consensus eigengene networks capture the relationships among consensus
modules; the relationships are quantified by eigengene correlations.

Built with R version `r getRversion()`

## R Preliminaries

Environment cleaning and set-up

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
library(WGCNA)

# The following setting is important, do not omit.
options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
# Enabling multithreading
enableWGCNAThreads() # Seems to work in RStudio
```

## Load data

```{r load}
rm(list = ls())

# Basic settings: we work with two data sets
nSets = 4
# For easier labeling of plots, create a vector holding descriptive names of the two sets.
setLabels = c('a554', 's018', 's311', 's84')

# Input data for analysis
load('../rdata-saved/wgcna/Consensus-dataInput-MAD025.RData')

# wgcna results
load('../rdata-saved/wgcna/Consensus-NetworkConstruction-ctrl-man13mrg025.RData')
```

We have loaded the variables `multiExpr` and `Traits` containing the expression and trait data, respectively. Further, expression data dimensions are stored in `nGenes` and `nSamples`. The consensus network analysis results are represented by the variables `consMEs`, `moduleLabels`, `moduleColors`, and `consTree`.

## Comparing eigengene networks

In this section we compare the consensus eigengene networks between data sets for lines (such comparison
is often called differential analysis). Consensus eigengene networks capture the relationships among consensus
modules; the relationships are quantified by eigengene correlations:

```{r weight as eigengene}
#| output: false
#| message: false
# Create a variable weight that will hold just the body weight of mice in all sets
time = vector(mode = "list", length = nSets)
for (set in 1:nSets)
{
time[[set]] = list(data = as.data.frame(Traits[[set]]$data$time))
names(time[[set]]$data) = "time"
}
# Recalculate consMEs to give them color names
consMEsC = multiSetMEs(multiExpr, universalColors = moduleColors)
# We add the time trait to the eigengenes and order them by consensus hierarchical clustering:
MET = consensusOrderMEs(addTraitToMEs(consMEsC, time))
```

We now call the function `plotEigengeneNetworks` that performs the differential analysis:

```{r differential analysis, fig.width=8, fig.height=10, dpi=300, dev='png'}
pdf(file = "preservation_analysis13mrg025.pdf", width = 24, height = 24)
par(cex = 0.9)
plotEigengeneNetworks(MET, setLabels, marDendro = c(0,2,2,1), marHeatmap = c(3,3,2,1),
zlimPreservation = c(0.5, 1), xLabelsAngle = 90)
dev.off()

```

## References
